species: Low-frequency Whale + Volcano Infrasonic Dual -- two planetary infrasonic sources simultaneously, deep Pacific
scientific_name: Balaenoptera musculus (Blue Whale, Mysticeti) + Balaenoptera physalus (Fin Whale, Mysticeti) + submarine volcanic seismic tremor (abiotic, Juan de Fuca Ridge / Pacific Ring of Fire). No single taxonomic designation for the combined event.
sound_type: Infrasonic dual planetary. Two sources: (1) Baleen whale vocalization -- Blue Whale 14-100+ Hz (lowest calls <20 Hz infrasonic), amplitude 155-188 dB re 1 microPa; Fin Whale 20-Hz pulses at 0.5-1 Hz repetition. SOFAR channel propagation 1000+ km. (2) Submarine volcanic seismic tremor -- Juan de Fuca Ridge and Pacific Ring of Fire systems, 0.5-10 Hz, fully infrasonic, continuous during seismic episodes. Combined frequency range 0.5-200 Hz. Both sources propagate through SOFAR channel. Frequency overlap: Blue Whale lowest calls overlap with top of volcanic tremor range (~10-20 Hz).
ecosystem: Deep Pacific -- pelagic and abyssal zones, SOFAR channel (700-1200 m depth), submarine volcanic systems. Juan de Fuca Ridge: 300-500 km off Washington coast, active volcanic spreading center. Axial Seamount: 480 km off Washington coast, erupted 1998, 2011, 2015. Eastern North Pacific Blue Whale migration route passes through offshore Washington zone.
season: Rare. Not seasonal but episodic. Requires simultaneous active volcanic tremor (during seismic episode/eruption) and active baleen whale vocalization in same acoustic zone at maximum amplitude. Axial Seamount eruption events (1998, 2011, 2015) represent maximum expression moments. Between eruptions: low-level volcanic tremor continuous; whale calls always present in Pacific. Full E=9 requires both at maximum simultaneously.
family: Multi-species. Blue Whale Balaenoptera musculus (Order Cetacea, Suborder Mysticeti, Family Balaenopteridae). Fin Whale Balaenoptera physalus (same family). Plus abiotic submarine volcanic seismic source (no taxonomy).
milestone: THE HEAVIEST POSSIBLE SPS ENTRY. THE ENGINE'S MAXIMUM NATURAL ACOUSTIC EVENT. Paired with Melvins (S36-334) at E=9 + E=9 = E=18: THE HEAVIEST DEGREE IN THE ENGINE. Two planetary infrasonic sources at maximum. Frequency range 0.5-200 Hz covers the absolute floor of the acoustic spectrum. Deepest, heaviest, most infrasonic entry in the SPS engine. SOFAR channel provides planetary-scale propagation for both sources.

coordinates.description: Representative: Axial Seamount / Juan de Fuca Ridge axis, 46°N 130°W. Deep Pacific pelagic zone 300-500 km offshore Washington/Oregon. SOFAR channel depth here approximately 900 m. Eastern North Pacific Blue Whale migration route overlaps with Juan de Fuca Ridge acoustic zone.

acoustic_data.source_1_whale: Baleen whale: Blue Whale 155-188 dB re 1 microPa. Frequency: 14-100 Hz core, lowest calls <20 Hz infrasonic. Fin Whale: 20-Hz pulses at 0.5-1 Hz repetition rate. SOFAR channel propagation 1000+ km. Eastern North Pacific Blue Whale population ~2000 animals (endangered, recovering since 1986 moratorium).
acoustic_data.source_2_volcano: Submarine volcanic tremor: Juan de Fuca Ridge / Axial Seamount. Frequency: 0.5-10 Hz, fully infrasonic. Continuous during active seismic episodes. Seafloor-to-water-column coupling at deep Pacific floor. Detected at shore seismometer networks (Olympic Peninsula). Eruption events 1998, 2011, 2015.
acoustic_data.combined: Combined frequency range: 0.5-200 Hz. Infrasonic content: geological (0.5-10 Hz) + biological low edge (10-20 Hz) continuously occupies the infrasonic-to-low-frequency transition zone. SOFAR channel propagates both sources simultaneously. E=9 through simultaneous planetary infrasonic maximum: pressure felt, not sound heard.
acoustic_data.e9_calibration: E=9 through planetary infrasonic simultaneous maximum. Blue Whale alone ~E=7-8; volcanic tremor alone ~E=7; combined simultaneous at Axial Seamount eruption with calling whales nearby = E=9. The deepest, heaviest acoustic event in the SPS engine. Not amplitude maximum but frequency-floor maximum.

nodes.title: Infrasonic Dual Planetary -- Deepest Entry in SPS Engine
nodes.description: Two planetary infrasonic sources simultaneously: baleen whale vocalization (10-200 Hz, lowest calls infrasonic) and submarine volcanic seismic tremor (0.5-10 Hz, fully infrasonic). Combined 0.5-200 Hz. Both propagate via SOFAR channel. E=9 through planetary infrasonic maximum. THE HEAVIEST NATURAL ACOUSTIC EVENT IN THE ENGINE.

nodes.title: SOFAR Channel -- Planetary Acoustic Waveguide
nodes.description: Sound Fixing and Ranging channel: 700-1200 m depth, minimum sound velocity. Traps low-frequency sound and propagates it at planetary distances with minimal absorption. Baleen whales use it for long-distance communication. Submarine volcanic seismic converts to acoustic in this waveguide. Both infrasonic sources use the same propagation mechanism.

nodes.title: Frequency Overlap: Blue Whale Call Base + Volcanic Tremor Top
nodes.description: Blue Whale lowest calls (below 20 Hz) overlap with volcanic tremor frequency range top (~10-20 Hz). The two sources share the 10-20 Hz zone. Combined with the rest of each source's range: 0.5-200 Hz continuous, with the geological floor (0.5-10 Hz) and the biological range (10-200 Hz) connected by the shared infrasonic zone.

nodes.title: Axial Seamount -- The Maximum Expression Site
nodes.description: 480 km off Washington coast. Active submarine volcano on Juan de Fuca Ridge. Erupted 1998, 2011, 2015. During eruption: seismic swarm plus volcanic tremor at maximum amplitude. Eastern North Pacific Blue Whale migration route passes nearby. Eruption coincidence with nearby calling whales = maximum E=9 expression of infrasonic dual.

nodes.title: Blue Whale -- Loudest Biological Sound on Earth
nodes.description: 155-188 dB re 1 microPa at 1 meter. Among the loudest biological sounds ever recorded. Eastern North Pacific population ~2000 animals, endangered, recovering since 1986 whaling moratorium. Calls propagate 1000+ km via SOFAR channel. The biological component of the planetary infrasonic dual.

cross_references.relationship: E=9 + E=9 = E=18. THE ENGINE'S MAXIMUM ENERGY DEGREE. Melvins sludge 40-100 Hz (sub-bass adjacency to infrasonic) meets whale-volcano infrasonic 0.5-200 Hz. Frequency ranges overlap. Human music most adjacent to infrasonic domain paired with actual infrasonic planetary dual. Sludge metal = the weight of the deep Pacific in human music.

E=8 opens the SPS summit arc; E=9 escalates it. Full-spectrum storm (all frequency domains, E=8) to single-mechanism infrasonic maximum (E=9). The storm activates the full range; the whale-volcano occupies the deepest level.

Both involve cetacean vocalization in Pacific waters. Humpback+Orca (E=9) is multi-species convergence in the same acoustic channel. Whale+Volcano (E=9) is cetacean vocalization combined with geological infrasonic source. Two different dimensions of the deep Pacific acoustic environment, both at maximum energy.
